Estun Automation Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2715) ) has issued an update.

Estun Automation Co., Ltd. has announced the current composition of its board of directors, detailing four executive directors, one non-executive director, and three independent non-executive directors. The roles include Mr. Wu Bo as chairman of the board, supported by executive, non-executive, and independent directors who collectively oversee corporate governance.

The company has also clarified membership and leadership across its five key board committees: Audit, Remuneration and Appraisal, Nomination, Strategic, and ESG. By specifying which directors chair and sit on each committee, Estun provides investors with greater transparency into oversight of financial reporting, strategy, executive pay, and sustainability matters.

The group operates in the automation and robotics sector, supplying motion-control technologies, robotic solutions, and related systems to manufacturing customers seeking smarter, more efficient production lines.

data.title: SIIC Environment Issues Pro Forma Update and Corrects Currency Errors on Acquisition
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SIIC Environment Holdings ( (SG:BHK) ) has shared an update.

SIIC Environment Holdings has issued a supplemental announcement related to its previously disclosed acquisition of 100% equity interests in certain target companies. The company has prepared unaudited pro forma financial information to illustrate the potential impact of the acquisition on its financial position as at 31 December 2025 and its 2025 statement of comprehensive income, using accounting policies consistent with its latest annual report.

The group also clarified clerical errors in an earlier announcement regarding the presentation currency of net tangible assets, profit attributable to shareholders, net tangible assets per share, and earnings per share. These amounts should have been denominated in RMB instead of RMB’000, and the per-share figures in RMB instead of RMB cents, with the revised pro forma statement detailing adjusted cash, receivables, and concession assets following the acquisition and related consideration.

More about SIIC Environment Holdings

SIIC Environment Holdings Ltd. is a Singapore-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong and Singapore that operates in the environmental services sector. The group focuses on water and wastewater treatment and related infrastructure projects, generating revenue from concession arrangements and associated environmental services in mainland China and the region.

China Resources Power Holdings Co ( (HK:0836) ) has shared an update.

China Resources Power Holdings disclosed key financial figures for its subsidiary China Resources Power Investment Company Limited for 2025 and the first quarter of 2026, as required by PRC regulations. CRP Investment reported audited 2025 revenue of RMB55.45 billion and net profit of RMB5.67 billion, with total assets of RMB114.84 billion at year-end and a net cash increase of RMB2.39 billion.

For the first three months of 2026, the subsidiary posted unaudited revenue of RMB14.04 billion and net profit of RMB1.22 billion, alongside a notable rise in cash and cash equivalents to RMB7.38 billion. The company stressed that these figures, prepared under PRC GAAP and subject to possible audit adjustments, reflect only the subsidiary’s performance and do not provide a complete picture of the wider group, prompting investors to exercise caution when assessing the group’s securities.

BBMG ( (HK:2009) ) has provided an update.

BBMG Corporation has announced a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.05 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025 and updated the ex-dividend date to 17 June 2026. The record date is set for 29 June 2026, with payment scheduled for 31 July 2026, and the Hong Kong dollar amount and exchange rate will be confirmed later.

The company detailed the withholding tax arrangements for different shareholder groups, including a 10% enterprise income tax for non-resident enterprises and a 20% individual income tax for Mainland investors accessing H shares through Shanghai-Hong Kong Stock Connect. These arrangements clarify net dividend expectations for various investor categories and may influence after-tax returns and portfolio planning for both overseas and domestic shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2009) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BBMG stock, see the HK:2009 Stock Forecast page.

The latest announcement is out from Anchorstone Holdings Limited ( (HK:1592) ).

Anchorstone Holdings Limited, a Cayman-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1592, operates through a group structure with oversight from a board of directors that includes independent non-executive members. The firm follows standard Hong Kong corporate governance practices, including the engagement of external auditors and the use of shareholder mandates for capital management.

The company has called its annual general meeting for 18 June 2026 in Hong Kong to adopt the 2025 audited financial statements, reappoint Guangshen (Hong Kong) CPA Limited as auditor, and re-elect two independent non-executive directors with board authority over directors’ pay. Shareholders will also vote on renewing general mandates allowing the board to issue up to 20% new shares and to repurchase up to 10% of issued shares, providing management with flexibility for future financing and share buybacks within prescribed limits.

These resolutions, if approved, will maintain the company’s existing governance framework and preserve tools for active capital structure management. The mandates are time-limited until the next annual general meeting or earlier revocation, ensuring periodic shareholder oversight of equity issuance and repurchase activities.

More about Anchorstone Holdings Limited

The group operates through a corporate structure that includes various subsidiaries and is overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting typical governance arrangements for mid-sized listed firms in the region.

Its operations are subject to Hong Kong listing rules and corporate governance requirements, and it engages external auditors for its consolidated financial statements. data.title: Fosun Pharma Subsidiary Wins Approval for Sodium Nitroprusside Injection
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shanghai Fosun Pharmaceutical (Group) Co ( (HK:2196) ) has issued an update.

Shanghai Fosun Pharmaceutical announced that its subsidiary Yaopharma Co., Ltd. has received approval from the National Medical Products Administration for the drug registration of Sodium Nitroprusside Injection. The product is indicated for hypertensive emergencies, including hypertensive crises and perioperative blood pressure control, as well as acute heart failure such as acute pulmonary edema and heart failure associated with acute myocardial infarction or valve insufficiency, expanding the subsidiary’s cardiovascular treatment portfolio and strengthening the group’s position in critical-care therapeutics.

The approval of Sodium Nitroprusside Injection enriches Fosun Pharma’s hospital-use injectable offerings, particularly in the cardiovascular and acute care segment. This development is expected to support the company’s competitiveness in China’s high-demand market for emergency cardiovascular drugs and may enhance its relationships with medical institutions that rely on stable supplies of essential critical-care medications.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2196) stock is a Buy with a HK$35.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shanghai Fosun Pharmaceutical (Group) Co stock, see the HK:2196 Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Cloudbreak Pharma Wins FDA Safety Nod to Advance Presbyopia Drug CBT-199
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Cloudbreak Pharma, Inc. ( (HK:2592) ) has issued an update.

Cloudbreak Pharma has announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has completed its safety review of the company’s Investigational New Drug application for CBT-199, clearing the way for proposed clinical investigations to proceed. CBT-199 is designed as a topical ophthalmic emulsion for presbyopia that uses a parasympathomimetic miotic agent to constrict the pupil and create a pinhole effect, improving near vision while leveraging a water-free formulation to enhance stability and dosing comfort.

The company describes the completion of the FDA safety review as a critical milestone in CBT-199’s clinical development and says it will continue to monitor progress and update shareholders and potential investors as appropriate. The development underscores Cloudbreak Pharma’s bid to strengthen its position in the ophthalmic therapeutics market, though the company notes there is no assurance that CBT-199 or other candidates will reach successful commercialization, a caveat that highlights ongoing clinical and regulatory risk for stakeholders.

More about Cloudbreak Pharma, Inc.

Cloudbreak Pharma Inc. is a Cayman Islands–incorporated biopharmaceutical company focused on ophthalmology, developing innovative topical treatments for age-related eye conditions. Its pipeline includes CBT-199, a novel, water-free ophthalmic emulsion formulated using a proprietary non-aqueous platform to address presbyopia, targeting global markets where near-vision impairment is increasingly prevalent.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

data.title: China Metal Resources Faces Going-Concern Disclaimer as Debt Restructuring Hinges on New Capital
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Metal Resources Utilization Ltd. ( (HK:1636) ) just unveiled an update.

China Metal Resources Utilization Limited has disclosed that its auditors issued a disclaimer of opinion on its 2024 financial statements due to going concern uncertainties, and the company is working to address this by pursuing a comprehensive debt restructuring. Management says the key prerequisite is securing new investment of between RMB300 million and RMB500 million, and three potential Sichuan-based investors have completed due diligence and are conducting internal assessments, while a separate RMB400 million convertible bond from Kaiyue remains delayed by liquidity issues.

The company continues negotiations with banks and tax authorities on restructuring existing debts but expects no definitive arrangements until fresh capital is raised, business operations normalize and major state-owned creditors agree to convert debt into equity. It has tightened cost control and working capital management and notes that the main operating subsidiaries’ bank accounts and assets have been unfrozen, meaning operations can return to normal once funding is in place, though further progress is not expected until the debt restructuring formally moves ahead.

More about China Metal Resources Utilization Ltd.

China Metal Resources Utilization Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands that operates in the metal resources sector. The group focuses on metal-related businesses in mainland China, and its operations depend heavily on stable financing, access to working capital and cooperative relationships with state-owned creditors and banks.

Average Trading Volume: 420,166

The latest announcement is out from Shaw Brothers Holdings Limited ( (HK:0953) ).

Shaw Brothers Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 11 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated accounts, the directors’ report and the independent auditor’s report for the year ended 31 December 2025. The agenda also includes re-appointment of SHINEWING (HK) CPA Limited as auditor, re-election of two independent non-executive directors, and authorisation for the board to fix directors’ and auditor’s remuneration.

Shareholders will further vote on granting the board a general mandate to allot, issue and deal with additional shares, including through offers or convertible instruments, up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital excluding treasury shares. This mandate, which runs until the next annual general meeting or its statutory due date, gives management greater flexibility to raise capital or pursue strategic transactions while remaining subject to standard exclusions such as rights issues, employee share schemes and scrip dividend arrangements.

More about Shaw Brothers Holdings Limited

Shaw Brothers Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, engaged in business activities that require regular shareholder oversight through annual general meetings. The company is governed by a board of directors and independent non-executive directors, and its financial reporting is audited by SHINEWING (HK) CPA Limited.

Current Market Cap: HK$310.9M

data.title: Carote Updates Timetable for 2025 Final Dividend Payout
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Carote Ltd ( (HK:2549) ) has shared an update.

Carote Ltd has proposed a final ordinary cash dividend of HKD 0.1419 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, subject to shareholder approval at a meeting scheduled for 29 May 2026. The dividend will be paid in Hong Kong dollars, with an ex-dividend date of 6 July 2026, a record date of 10 July 2026, and a scheduled payment date of 31 July 2026.

The announcement mainly updates key timetable details for investors, including the latest time to lodge share transfer documents on 7 July 2026 and the book closure period from 8 to 10 July 2026 for determining dividend entitlements. This clarification provides shareholders and potential investors with greater visibility on the company’s capital return schedule and supports more precise planning around trading and corporate actions tied to the dividend.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2549) stock is a Hold with a HK$4.64 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Carote Ltd stock, see the HK:2549 Stock Forecast page.

More about Carote Ltd

Carote Ltd is a Hong Kong-listed equity issuer, with its shares traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The company is overseen by a board comprising executive directors and independent non-executive directors, reflecting standard Hong Kong corporate governance practices for listed firms.
